Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Request For Documents And, In The Alternative, For An Order Compelling Compliance On Plaintiff'S Failure To Permit Inspection In Accordance With Responses; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-03-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Sam Wilkins' Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Wilkins' Request For Documents and, in the alternative, For An Order Compelling Compliance On Plaintiff's Failure To Permit Inspection In Accordance With Responses is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted in part and denied in part as to No. 30. The motion is granted as the motion seeks all communications, including emails, as those communications relate to the establishment and operation of the PFI policy. This order does not include communications constituting or relating to the solicitation of investments. The relevant inquiry is the establishment and implementation of the PFI policy as it applies to the Autostar investment. That inquiry does not include every aspect of the investment. Any responsive documents subject to a claim of privilege shall be identified with particularity. Objections are otherwise overruled.
The motion is granted as to No. 33 as it seeks notes, agendas, memoranda, policies, proposals, resolutions, and minutes of the meetings chaired by Wilkens. As the motion seeks all communications among or between PMP members or employees, or PMP meeting attendees, the motion is denied. The court does not see that anything else is requested or in dispute. Objections are otherwise overruled.
The same rulings, as made as to No. 33, are made as to Nos. 3, 5, 15, 16, and 17. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege shall be identified with particularity.
Further production shall be made within 20 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
